channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tanzania PM mediates settlement for 51-year land conflict in Kilimanjaro
item.description: Tanzania’s Prime Minister, Mwigulu Nchemba, successfully mediated a settlement for a land dispute that has troubled Msaranga Village, Rombo District, in Kilimanjaro Region for more than five decades. ...

item.title: Pakistan and Iran discuss diplomatic efforts to ease US-Iran tensions and prevent regional escalation
item.description: Pakistan’s Chief of Defence Forces Field Marshal Syed Asim Munir met Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi in Tehran in a high-level diplomatic engagement focused on reducing tensions linked to ongo...

item.title: Nigerian banking elites increase London property investments amid currency risks and UK tax changes
item.description: Top Nigerian banking executives and business elites are increasingly putting money into London real estate, even as the United Kingdom raises taxes on luxury homes and tightens property-related regula...

item.title: Melbourne teenager sets Australian record as youngest climber to reach Mount Everest summit
item.description: An 18-year-old from Melbourne, Bianca Adler, has become the youngest Australian to successfully summit Mount Everest, the world’s highest peak at 8,849 metres. She completed the ascent on her second a...

item.title: Berlin AI startup Peec surpasses $10 million in annualized revenue as demand for AI search optimization grows
item.description: Berlin-based startup Peec AI has reportedly surpassed $10 million in annualized recurring revenue only months after reporting more than $4 million in revenue growth following its launch. According to ...

item.title: Dutch traveller intercepted at OR Tambo Airport with 40kg of khat bound for Germany
item.description: A Dutch national has been arrested at OR Tambo International Airport in Johannesburg after being found in possession of more than 40 kilograms of khat during an intelligence-driven operation conducted...

item.title: Discussion on Teachers’ Hesitation to Discipline Students on ‘It’s Showtime’
item.description: In a recent segment on the popular Philippine variety show 'It’s Showtime', the topic of teachers being hesitant to discipline students was highlighted. The show discussed concerns among educators who...

item.title: LTFRB clarifies: PUV service contracting subsidies already released to operators; drivers must be paid through them
item.description: The Land Transportation Franchising and Regulatory Board (LTFRB) clarified that payments under the government’s Service Contracting Program (SCP) for public utility vehicle (PUV) drivers have already ...

item.title: Lagos records 1,437 child protection cases, carries out 5,708 interventions — Commissioner
item.description: The Lagos State Government don step up efforts to fight child abuse, neglect, trafficking, and exploitation, as the state Child Protection Unit records 1,437 reported cases within the review period. A...

item.title: Letters to the editor highlight concerns about public health trust, governance, and global issues
item.description: This collection of letters to the editor published on May 23 presents a range of reader perspectives on international politics, Canadian institutions, public health, and social trends. A central contr...

item.title: North Melbourne defeat Gold Coast after siren goal by Cam Zurhaar sparks controversy
item.description: North Melbourne has secured a dramatic and contentious AFL victory over the Gold Coast Suns after Cam Zurhaar kicked a goal following the final siren. The match concluded in highly charged circumstanc...

item.title: Australia’s economy slows as unemployment rises, tax reforms spark debate, and investors eye space industry growth
item.description: Australia’s economy is showing clearer signs of cooling, with new labour market data indicating a rise in unemployment to 4.5 per cent in April, the highest level since late 2021. Nearly 19,000 jobs w...

item.title: Why Silicon Valley Billionaires Are Investing in Survival Bunkers and Future Uncertainty
item.description: This opinion article by Osmund Agbo looks at the growing trend among Silicon Valley billionaires who are quietly preparing for possible global or societal collapse. It highlights how figures like Mark...

item.title: The Story Behind Harris Lyons’ Dual Mugshots and His 1893 Criminal Arrest
item.description: The article explores the unusual case of Harris Lyons, a criminal arrested in 1893 whose police records included two nearly identical mugshots taken in Newark, New Jersey. The photographs, preserved a...

item.title: Tanzania Considers Using University Certificates as Collateral for Youth Loans
item.description: Tanzania government iko kwenye process ya kuandaa sheria itakayowezesha graduates wa vyuo vikuu kutumia academic certificates zao kama collateral pale wanapoiomba mikopo ya biashara. Deputy Minister f...

item.title: Spanish Artist Josep Renau and His Legacy of Political Photomontage and Anti-Fascist Art
item.description: The article explores the life and artistic legacy of Josep Renau, a Spanish artist, graphic designer, and political activist whose work became deeply connected to social criticism and anti-fascist res...

item.title: Tim Hortons to Expand with 80 New Locations Across Canada and Renovate Existing Stores
item.description: Tim Hortons, one of Canada's most iconic coffee and fast-food chains, has announced plans to open 80 new restaurants nationwide, along with renovations to existing locations. The expansion is part of ...

item.title: Increased maritime traffic around the Cape of Good Hope raises whale collision risks
item.description: Scientists are warning of a growing risk to whales along South Africa’s west coast as global shipping routes are increasingly diverted around the Cape of Good Hope. This shift is largely driven by geo...

item.title: San Diego Mosque Shooting Linked to Prior Warning Signs and Seized Firearms
item.description: One of the teenagers involved in this week’s deadly shooting at the Islamic Center of San Diego had previously exhibited alarming behavior, including idolizing Nazis and mass shooters, which prompted ...

item.title: Kathy Andrews Praises Sister Doreen Makena for Buying First Car After Overcoming Family Truggles
item.description: Nairobi businesswoman Kathy Andrews has touched many Kenyans online after celebrating her elder sister, Doreen Makena, for buying her first car. In an emotional social media tribute, Kathy explained t...
